lede: A look at July 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Giuseppe Garibaldi and Tomaž Šalamun, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 1982 kidnapping of Iranian diplomats
events.desc: Three Iranian diplomats and a journalist are kidnapped in Lebanon by Phalange forces, and their fate remains unknown.

events.title: Israel Defense Forces
events.desc: Israeli commandos raid Entebbe airport in Uganda, rescuing all but four of the passengers and crew of an Air France jetliner seized by Palestinian terrorists.

events.title: Barbados
events.desc: Barbados, Guyana, Jamaica, and Trinidad and Tobago sign the Treaty of Chaguaramas in Chaguaramas, Trinidad and Tobago establishing the Caribbean Community (CARICOM). It replaces the Caribbean Free Trade Association as another step towards Caribbean regional integration.

events.title: William Shockley
events.desc: William Shockley announces the invention of the junction transistor.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Burning of the Riga synagogues: The Great Choral Synagogue in German-occupied Riga is burnt with 300 Jews locked in the basement.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Battle of Hamel, a successful attack by the Australian Corps against German positions near the town of Le Hamel on the Western Front.

events.title: Alabama
events.desc: In Alabama, the Tuskegee Institute opens.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Siege of Vicksburg: The Confederate army in Vicksburg, Mississippi surrenders to Union forces under Ulysses S. Grant after 47 days of siege, contributing to the Union capture of the Mississippi River.

events.title: Grand Junction Railway
events.desc: Grand Junction Railway, the world's first long-distance railway, opens between Birmingham and Liverpool.

events.title: Theodosius II
events.desc: Emperor Theodosius II, age 13, yields power to his older sister Aelia Pulcheria, who reigned as regent and proclaimed herself empress (Augusta) of the Eastern Roman Empire.

births.name: Calvin Coolidge
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 30th President of the United States

births.name: Gackt
births.line: Japanese musician, singer, songwriter, record producer and actor

births.name: Oscar I of Sweden
births.line: King of Sweden and Norway from 1844 to 1859

deaths.text: Władysław Sikorski, Polish general and politician, 9th Prime Minister of the Second Republic of Poland
Otto Bauer, Austrian philosopher and politician, Austrian Minister of Foreign Affairs
Marie Curie, French-Polish physicist and chemist, Nobel Prize laureate
James Monroe, American soldier, lawyer, and politician, 5th President of the United States
John Adams, American lawyer and politician, 2nd President of the United States
Thomas Jefferson, American architect, lawyer, and politician, 3rd President of the United States

observances.text: Christian feast day: Saint Pier Giorgio Frassati
Christian feast day: Elizabeth of Aragon (or of Portugal)
